title: Allospondias laxiflora
taxonomy: Allospondias laxiflora is a species of plant in the family Anacardiaceae. This family includes various species of trees and shrubs commonly known as the cashew family. Allospondias laxiflora is a flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Anacardiaceae family, Allospondias laxiflora is classified as a member of the genus Allospondias. The specific classification of Allospondias laxiflora is as a species of deciduous shrub or small tree.

Its leaves and bark are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. intro: Allospondias laxiflora, also known as the African pear or bush mango, is a tropical evergreen tree native to West Africa, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae.
